claims.text: Langfuse was founded in 2022 by Marc Klingen, Clemens Rawert, and Max Deichmann.
claims.evidence: Founded 2022 by Marc Klingen, Clemens Rawert, and Max Deichmann; went through Y Combinator (W23).

claims.text: Marc Klingen is listed as CEO at Langfuse.
claims.evidence: Langfuse generates an estimated $1.1M in annual revenue. Langfuse was founded by Marc Klingen.

claims.text: Langfuse was accepted into Y Combinator (W23) in November 2022.
claims.evidence: While our customers were excited about Finto (the billing product), we realized that we were not excited about building the *nth* generation of product in an established (small) market.

claims.text: Langfuse has raised a total of $4.5 million over 2 funding rounds, with the first round in 2023.
claims.evidence: Langfuse has raised a total funding of $4.5M over 2 rounds. Its first funding round was on 2023.

claims.text: Langfuse generates an estimated $1.1 million in annual revenue as of 2024.
claims.evidence: 2024 | Langfuse Hit $1.1m revenue in June 2024 | Estimated.

claims.text: Claimed full-time founder of Langfuse since 2022.
claims.evidence: Langfuse is an open-source LLM engineering platform founded in 2022.

claims.text: Langfuse was established as a response to the challenges in debugging and improving production-grade LLM applications.
claims.evidence: Our goal is to enable product and engineering teams to harness the power of Generative AI with the most useful and open suite of devtools focused on visibility and insights.

claims.text: Claimed full-time CEO of Langfuse overlaps with claimed Y Combinator cohort tenure in 2023.
claims.evidence: Langfuse was founded in 2022 by Marc Klingen, Clemens Rawert, and Max Deichmann; went through Y Combinator (W23).
